output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 3/3.
Batch 1: Implemented batch 1. T1 now invalidates active shot queries after shot creation, and T2 now persists final-video dismissals across remounts while suppressing the badge when the final video is already on the timeline. Relevant Vitest modules passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 verification only. No additional source edits were needed: the old API names have no remaining runtime consumers in `src/`, `TimelineEditor.tsx` is the only runtime hook consumer, and `TimelineCanvas.tsx` plus `ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx` still receive callbacks through unchanged prop shapes. Wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 verification. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly, a temporary verification test module was created under `src/`, run successfully, and removed, and the final diff review confirms the intended source changes address all three bugs. Batch checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-final-video-20260406-0959/execution_batch_3.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed the inactive-only invalidation filter from both shot creation paths. Verified the active ShotsContext query uses the same ['shots', projectId, maxImagesPerShot] prefix that invalidateShotsQueries targets, so invalidateQueries now refetches the mounted list query as intended.

Moved final-video dismissal state to a module-level Set keyed by finalVideo.id and rewired TimelineEditor to suppress badges when a current row clip already references that final video generation. Verified the switch chain remains consistent: useSwitchToFinalVideo registers generationId=finalVideo.id, stores the asset on the inserted clip meta, and the new rows->meta->registry scan detects it.

Verified the API rename blast radius by searching for dismissShot, dismissedShotIds, and useFinalVideoAvailable across src/. No remaining old-name consumers exist. The hook itself is only imported by TimelineEditor.tsx for runtime use; TimelineCanvas.tsx and ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx continue to receive callbacks via props, and the relevant timeline editor test modules pass with the current prop signatures.

Ran `npx tsc --noEmit` successfully with no type errors. Wrote and ran a temporary verification test module under `src/` to exercise the three fixed behaviors, then removed it after it passed. Final review of the remaining diff shows only the intended source changes for shot invalidation, final-video dismissal persistence, and timeline-based final-video badge suppression; the only unrelated dirty file is the pre-existing `.megaplan/debt.json`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed by inspection: ShotsContext mounts useListShots with queryKey ['shots', projectId, maxImagesPerShot], and invalidateShotsQueries invalidates ['shots', projectId] without a refetchType override. TanStack Query will therefore mark the active shots list stale and refetch it.

Confirmed by tracing the code path: useSwitchToFinalVideo creates the replacement asset with generationId: finalVideo.id, stores that asset key on the new clip's meta entry, and resolveFinalVideoShotIds now scans rows -> meta[action.id].asset -> registry.assets[assetKey].generationId, so a placed final video is detected from actual timeline clips.

Confirmed. Runtime hook usage is limited to TimelineEditor.tsx, with useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts and TimelineEditor.test.ts only importing the shared ShotFinalVideo type. TimelineCanvas.tsx and ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx accept callback props with the same shotId/group shapes as before, so the rename did not change their prop contracts. TimelineCanvas.test.tsx and TimelineEditor.test.ts both pass.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly. The temporary verification module also passed and confirmed the three targeted behaviors: active shots queries refetch after invalidation, dismissed final-video ids persist in module scope and do not suppress newer final videos for the same shot, and the badge is hidden only when a current timeline clip is using the final video generation id.
